programmes.show_description: Confessions of a Serial Killer with Piers Morgan: Piers comes face-to-face with Bernard Giles, who murdered five female hitch-hikers in late 1973.
It's Not All Beaches and Blue Skies: Anna hits the first milestone in Australia without the support of family: Isabelle's 8th birthday. Jay settles into work, but life at home is not so smooth.
Almost Feels Like Home: Maggie and Darren start to settle in at Perth's central station, but Anna struggles with her work-life balance. Michael gets a very unusual reptilian call out.
Shopping from Home.
Today the contestants must keep up with world-renowned chef Curtis Stone to win a 30-minute advantage in the semi-final.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien find a butter churn, a slot machine and a set of trays for storing old printing letters as they continue their mission to turn items into hard cash.
West Bromwich: Nick Knowles and the team, together with West Bromwich volunteers, help a grieving grandmother and her family who are making do in a very small house.
Grenfell Boxing Club: Nick Knowles and the team are constructing two buildings for the Grenfell community, starting with a new home for the Dale Youth Boxing Club.

Betsy & Angel: Cockapoo Betsy was hit by a van five months ago and has developed gangrene, while bite-size bijou Dogue de Bordeaux puppy Angel has a genetic deformity.
Saving Noel's Dog: When Noel's beloved Border Terrier Keira is hit by a van, he must face the possibility that she may not be fixable.
Barnet: Nick Knowles and the DIY SOS team transform the home of a wheelchair-bound police officer who was a victim of the Westminster Bridge terrorist attack in March 2017.
West Bromwich: Nick Knowles and the team, together with West Bromwich volunteers, help a grieving grandmother and her family who are making do in a very small house.
The team treat a lorry driver trapped behind his twisted steering wheel and help is urgently required for a 12-year-old who appears to be drunk after drinking an unknown substance.

programmes.show_name: How To Catch a Serial Killer
programmes.show_description: Former detective Steven Fulcher breaks down the risky investigation that caught infamous murderer Christopher Halliwell - and ended his policing career.
Ghost Ship: When a Bahamas-bound boat is found abandoned 140 miles off course, investigators wade through blood evidence and reports of a possible pirate attack as they look for answers.
Piers meets some of America's most notorious female killers to discover what drives them to kill, starting with Erin Caffey, who masterminded the murder of her family aged 16.
Piers continues his travels across the USA, this time meeting two very different female murderers, both of whom killed someone they loved.
No Longer Rookies: The Khan family have embraced life in Perth so fully that they've decided to make it permanent by purchasing land, while Anna is struggling with the shift hours.
Policing in Paradise: It's the end of probation and Maggie's dream of being a police dog handler is revived, while Michael returns to Perth from Halls Creek. Jay and his wife make a decision.
Shopping from Home.
It's nearing the end and all that stands between the semi-finalists and the Grand Finale is an epic three-course service challenge battle.
Paramedics in Stoke respond to a call about a man who has collapsed with severe breathlessness, while in Dudley, crews treat a woman with a host of complex medical issues.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Simon O'Brien repurposes some old suitcases, while Henry Cole gives an old dresser some serious bling.
Avening: The team helps project manager Ben in Avening in the Cotswolds, whose life was changed in a split second by a freak accident while holidaying in Cyprus.
Mildenhall: Nick Knowles meets a husband, father and football fan who was brain damaged in an attack, and now needs his home adapted for his care and rehabilitation.

Inca, Nala & Charles: Anxious two-year-old Cavapoo Nala arrives for a hip replacement, and Great Dane Charles was diagnosed a spinal condition known as Wobbler Syndrome.
Angus & Rocky: Goldendoodle Angus fell 20 metres and sustained nerve damage, while irrepressible Springer Spaniel Rocky's failed elbow operation has left him struggling to walk.
Grenfell Community Centre: Nick Knowles and the team continue their efforts at Grenfell by building a two storey flexible use centre to be used by the local community.
Avening: The team helps project manager Ben in Avening in the Cotswolds, whose life was changed in a split second by a freak accident while holidaying in Cyprus.
Mel, Murphy and Twist: It's Pete's first day at Dogs Trust, and he meets a pug facing a worrying surgery and pregnant dog who was seized at the border.
The Potato Pups: Pete meets a litter of puppies known as the Potato Pups, who were found abandoned in a crisp box, and helps the staff fight to keep them alive.
Killer Wave: When a woman is swept off her boat by a rogue wave in California, search teams race to find her before it's too late. But were rough seas the real culprit?

programmes.show_name: Deepfake Porn: Could You Be Next?
programmes.show_description: We look at the rising trend of harmless images being turned into hardcore porn - footage that is often used against the people depicted with devastating consequences.
